root.narration: The other team got a penalty, so your team has five skaters against their four. Your team is on the power play in the offensive zone. You have the puck at the top of the zone. The penalty killers are packed in tight around the net. What do you do?

choices.text: Move the puck around the outside to your teammates, making the penalty killers chase it until someone is open for a shot
Shoot the puck from the point through all the traffic in front
Skate straight into the group of penalty killers and try to force it through

outcome.headline: Power play goal!
outcome.explanation: By moving the puck around the outside, you made the four penalty killers shift from side to side. Eventually, a gap opened up and your teammate buried a one-timer. That is how a power play is supposed to work.
outcome.whatToRemember: On the power play, use your extra player by passing the puck quickly around the outside. Make the penalty killers move until they leave an opening. Patience and passing beat forcing it.

keyTerms.definition: When your team has more players on the ice because the other team has a player in the penalty box. Usually 5 skaters against 4.

outcome.headline: Shot blocked
outcome.explanation: Shooting from the point can work, but the penalty killers were blocking the lane. The puck hit a shin pad and bounced away. Getting shots through is good, but you had time to move the puck and create a better look.
outcome.whatToRemember: Point shots are useful on the power play, but first try to move the puck to open up shooting lanes. A clear path to the net is better than shooting into a wall of players.

outcome.headline: Shorthanded breakaway!
outcome.explanation: Skating into four defenders when you have open teammates is a bad idea. They stole the puck and one of their players broke out on a shorthanded breakaway. You almost gave up a goal while your team had the advantage.
outcome.whatToRemember: Never force the puck into traffic on the power play. You have the extra player, so use passing to create space. Forcing it can lead to shorthanded chances for the other team.
